bead.title: agent: run post-land commands outside the main-git lock
bead.description:
PROBLEM
A post-land command can run while `.ddx/.git-tracker.lock` is held, blocking every other worker/operator mutation for up to the post-land command timeout. This violates the TP-021 lock-lifetime contract: arbitrary commands must not execute while the main-git lock is held.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1208-1214 wraps all of `landLocked` in `withMainGitLock`.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1291 and :1369 call `preserveIfPostLandGateFails` inside that locked flow.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1445-1457 runs `PostLandCommand` with a 30 minute timeout.

PROPOSED FIX
- Refactor landing so post-land commands run after the main-git mutation lock is released, while preserving existing land/preserve semantics and evidence rewriting.
- If a post-land command fails, reacquire the lock only for the short preserve/ref/index mutations needed to record that failure.
- Add a channel-blocking post-land test proving another goroutine can acquire the main-git lock while the command is blocked.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove post-land command support.
- Do not change the 30 minute post-land command timeout unless a separate spec updates it.
bead.acceptance:
1. `TestChaos_PostLandCommandDoesNotHoldMainGitLock` blocks a post-land command and proves `withMainGitLock` can be acquired concurrently while the command is blocked.
2. Existing post-land failure preservation tests still pass, including `TestLand_PostLandCommandFailurePreserves` or the current equivalent in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go`.
3. Fast-forward and merge landing tests still pass after the refactor.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestChaos_PostLandCommandDoesNotHoldMainGitLock|TestLand_.*PostLand|TestLand_.*FastForward|TestLand_.*Merge'` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
